step_by_step_reasoning: We trace the architecture chronologically from the DNS edge servers uploading data to S3, through the ingestion pipeline driven by SNS and SQS to EC2-based transformers. The transformed raw parquet data in the S3 warehouse triggers aggregators via SNS to generate hourly/daily rollups catalogs in AWS Glue, which are queried by analysts and consumers using Amazon Athena.

graph.name: Verkara Scalable DNS Edge Network Analytics Pipeline
graph.notes: Ingests 20 TB of compressed data per day (6.6M records/second) from global edge DNS resolvers, processes it through EC2 transformers/aggregators to S3, cataloging it with AWS Glue, and querying via Amazon Athena to isolate workload query performance.

nodes.name: Global DNS Edge Resolvers
nodes.notes: Generates 530 billion transactions per day in compressed files.

nodes.name: S3 Ingest Bucket
nodes.notes: Receives 400,000 compressed files daily from edge locations.

nodes.name: Ingest SNS Topic
nodes.notes: Triggers notifications upon S3 file uploads.

nodes.name: Ingest SQS Queue
nodes.notes: Buffers incoming notification messages for transformers.

nodes.name: EC2 Transformers
nodes.notes: Converts proprietary compressed files to Parquet format.

nodes.name: S3 Warehouse Bucket
nodes.notes: Stores transformed parquet raw data and aggregated level tables.

nodes.name: Warehouse SNS Topic
nodes.notes: Triggers downstream aggregators upon raw file upload.

nodes.name: EC2 Aggregators
nodes.notes: Rolls up raw data into minute, hour, and day aggregations.

nodes.name: Glue Data Catalog
nodes.notes: Manages schema definitions for different aggregated tables.

nodes.name: Amazon Athena
nodes.notes: Executes isolated ad-hoc and reporting queries without performance degradation.

nodes.name: Internal Analysts
nodes.notes: Access reports and execute analytics queries.

edges.notes: Upload Parquet files into raw warehouse partition
S3 event notification for new raw files
Fetch raw data and save aggregated views back to warehouse S3 paths
